title: sida pedunculosa
intro: Sida pedunculosa, also known as the "country mallow" or "prairie mallow," is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States and the West Indies. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The medicinal properties of Sida pedunculosa have been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever reduction, and anti-cancer activities. Sida pedunculosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
